ENJOY DANCING EVENT At
FORT.


One of the very pleasant affairs
Of the season was the dance given
by the officers of Fort George
Wright and their wives in the gar
rison gymnasium last evening.
About rs were present, and a num-
Der of society folk from the city
journeyed out for the event. Holt
day decoration was the theme in
the gymnasium and patriotic ef-
feet was lent by draped American
flags. About Is were present and
greatly enjoyed the event.


HOLD BAZAAR FOR CHARITY
Members of the Hebrew Ladies'
Benevolent society are planning
for charity bazaar to be held in
a storeroom at 427 First avenue on
Monday. Stores of the city are do
mating articles for sale and many
booths will be attractively set up
and decorated for the TRACTION OF
the visitors. Mrs. L. Dollar has
charge of the arrangements with
the help of a committee.


WOMAN'S CLUB PLANS


TWO EVENTS IN NEW HOME


On Monday afternoon from 2 un-
til 5 o'clock the Woman's club will
hold a house warming and recep-
ton in their new clubhouse on
Ninth and Walnut, at which time
members of 20 other woman's clubs
of the city will be invited to in.
spect the appointments of the club
headquarters.


Tuesday evening the members of
the club will entertain their hus-
bands at a musicale and reception
at which time the Rev. Bruce
Brown will lecture on "Woman's
Work"
NEXT MEETING OF sCHU.


BERTS ON MONDAY


Members of the Schubert club
who gave their first concert last
evening. will meet in the Lillian
Courtney Snow hall, Hotel Tour
aine, on Monday. Pr


The charity vaudeville to be held
this evening in the Auditorium will
be participated in by many we'll
known society folk. Younger men,
bers of the society set are working
to make it large success and from
present indications will succeed.
